items.title: AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
items.summary: Rocket's new AI platform combines strategy, product building, and competitive intelligence, aiming to move beyond code generation.

items.title: AI design platform Picsart launches a creator monetization program
items.summary: The program invites creators to create original content with Picsart tools for a specific campaign, share it on their social channels, and earn revenue based on how their audience engages.

items.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
items.summary: Zero Shot, a new venture capital fund with deep ties to OpenAI, is aiming to raise $100 million for its first fund. It has already written some checks.

items.title: Gas prices aren’t the only factor fueling used EV sales
items.summary: The most recent electric vehicle sales data provided a grim picture — at least for new EVs. Used EVs are moving in the opposite direction.

items.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
items.summary: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

items.title: Netflix is expanding into kids’ games with a new stand-alone app
items.summary: Netflix says the app gives children access to an "ever-growing" library of games for kids.

items.title: Why safety regulators closed their investigation into Tesla’s remote parking feature
items.summary: The NHTSA closed its investigation into Tesla's "Actually Smart Summon" feature, saying that only a fraction of cases resulted in an incident and that no incidents resulted in injury. Tesla has also issued a number of software updates.

items.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
items.summary: Iran said it will target U.S.-linked data centers with new missile strikes, as the war between the U.S. and Iran escalates.

items.title: Apple moves to take its App Store fight back to the Supreme Court
items.summary: Apple plans to ask the Supreme Court to review its App Store fight with Epic Games, as it challenges a ruling limiting its ability to charge fees on external payments.

items.title: Watch this video of how a job interviewer exposes a North Korean fake IT worker
items.summary: An apparent North Korean worker was caught visibly stumped during a remote job interview when asked to insult the country's leader.

items.title: North Korea’s hijack of one of the web’s most used open source projects was likely weeks in the making
items.summary: North Korean hackers pushed out malicious updates to a popular open source project by hacking a top developer's computer in a long-running campaign.

items.title: OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
items.summary: OpenAI proposes taxes on AI profits, public wealth funds, and expanded safety nets to address job loss and inequality, blending redistribution with capitalism as policymakers debate AI’s economic impact.

items.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications open: a chance for VC access, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K
items.summary: Nominate your startup, or one you know that deserves the spotlight, and finish the process by applying. Selected 200 have a chance at VC access, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K for Startup Battlefield 200. Applications close on May 27.

items.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
items.summary: Learn how to use Spotify, Canva, Figma, Expedia, and other apps directly in ChatGPT.

items.title: Ticket savings of up to $500 this week for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
items.summary: Starting today, you have 5 days to save nearly $500 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. This offer disappears Friday, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here to secure these low rates.

items.title: Convicted spyware maker Bryan Fleming avoids jail at sentencing
items.summary: The pcTattletale founder escapes a custodial sentence following the first successful prosecution of a spyware maker in the U.S. in over a decade.

items.title: Spain’s Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI
items.summary: The company is also announcing a deal with L3Harris to build the sensors for Xoople's spacecraft.

items.title: Xiaomi 17 Ultra pushes smartphone photography even further
items.summary: The Xiaomi 17 Ultra gives you a ton of options to play around with images, including preset filters and hardware add-ons.

items.title: Polymarket took down wagers tied to rescue of downed Air Force officer
items.summary: A Democratic congressman had harsh criticism for Polymarket for allowing users to bet on the date the U.S. would confirm the rescue of Air Force service members shot down over Iran.

items.title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use
items.summary: AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.
